name: Dargavs Village
description: This ancient village and its adjoining cemetery have a beautiful history of death and remembrance.

name: Bunker-42
description: Dr. Strangelove-esque tours reveal the inside a bunker once reserved for Stalin himself.

name: Izmailovo Kremlin
description: Moscow's other, lesser known kremlin is a fairytale-like cultural complex modeled after Old Russia.

name: Moscow Metro Stations
description: The gorgeous entrances into the city's underbelly look more like grand halls than subway stops.

name: Lenin's Mausoleum
description: This building holds the embalmed remains of the leader of the Bolshevik Revolution.

name: Psychedelic Salt Mines
description: It's hard to believe the dizzying patterns covering this abandoned mine are completely natural.

name: Museum of Soviet Arcade Games
description: Immortal combat in a Moscow museum.

name: Laika Monument
description: It took over half a century but a Russian monument to a famous and tragic space pup finally stands in Moscow.

name: Children are the Victims of Adult Vices
description: Thirteen sculptures in central Moscow graphically depicting vices of the modern world.

name: Memorial Museum of Cosmonautics
description: Museum celebrating the achievements of the Soviet space program.

name: Rasputin Museum at Yusupov Palace
description: Palace where the nearly un-killable Rasputin was murdered.

name: Temple of All Religions
description: This Russian religious complex is a Frankenstein's chapel that includes influence from almost all the major faiths.

name: Kizhi Island
description: Stunning examples of Russia's 18th-century wooden architecture.

name: Church of Andrew the Apostle
description: A little church on a tiny island looks like something from a fairy tale.

name: Golosov Ravine
description: Legends of time travel and magical woodland creatures bolster the natural charm of this verdant sanctuary in the midst of Moscow.

name: The Amber Room
description: An 18th century wonder of the world, lost in WWII, re-created at the Catherine Palace.

name: Kunstkamera (Peter the Great Museum of Anthropology and Ethnography)
description: The oldest museum in Russia is home to a fascinating collection of nearly two million anthropological artifacts and anatomical curiosities.

name: House With Animals
description: Its front wall is striped with a menagerie of fantastic beasts and magnificent monsters.

name: Dancing Forest
description: The reason for the strange contortions in the trunks of these "drunken" pine trees in Russia is still a mystery.

name: The Irkutsk Babr
description: Thanks to a spelling mistake this bizarre beaver-tiger hybrid became the symbol of a Siberian town.

name: Fort Zverev
description: Nightmarish example of the unintended consequences of weapons testing.

name: Fallen Monument Park
description: A sculpture park of the tossed aside relics of the USSR, from Stalin to a massive Soviet emblem.

name: Russian Vodka Museum
description: The past, present, and future of Russia's signature alcohol is remembered in the world's only museum dedicated to the starchy spirit.

name: Lubyanka
description: Former headquarters of the KGB.

name: Oymyakon
description: The villagers of the coldest inhabited place on Earth adjust to unique challenges presented by day-to-day living.

name: Star City
description: Secret Soviet city once home to Russian cosmonauts and space training facilities.

name: Peacock Clock
description: A 250-year-old timepiece-automaton adorned with golden mechanical birds, which still sing to this day.

name: Novodevichy Cemetery
description: Moscow's most popular cemetery includes the graves of numerous cosmonauts.

name: Baikal Seals
description: Some of the world's only freshwater seals live in one very special lake in Russia.

name: Literary Café
description: Once frequented by heavyweights of the Russian arts scene, this café is stuck in the 1800s.

name: Miniature Moscow
description: Back in the tiny USSR.

name: Mir Diamond Mine
description: The world's second-largest excavated hole.

name: Monument to the Conquerors of Space
description: Moscow's imposing monument to the Space Age.

name: Lena Stone Pillars
description: A remote forest of stone pillars along the Lena river.

name: Tunguska Event Epicenter
description: Site of the largest impact event to occur over land in Earth's recorded history.

name: Sokol
description: An early-20th-century "dacha" village of quaint cottages curiously located in a modern part of Moscow.

name: Muromtzevo Castle
description: Abandoned castle was once a grand example of Russian privilege, built exclusively to win a frivolous bet between noblemen.

name: Hotel Ukraina
description: The tallest hotel in Europe.

name: Kadykchan
description: Siberian ghost town built by gulag prisoners.

name: Young Pioneer Camp
description: A Marxist summer camp full of fantastical statues lies tucked away and forgotten in the forests of Moscow.

name: Kaliningrad Oblast
description: A small part of Russia that does not touch any other part of Russia.

name: Tsar Bell
description: World's largest bell sits on the grounds of the Kremlin in Moscow.

name: Soviet Dacha
description: This frozen-in-time summer home outside Moscow provides a look at Russian life in the 1950s.

name: Freud's Dream Museum
description: Dark, eerie exhibits make for a surreal exploration of the subconscious.

name: Kola Superdeep Borehole
description: The deepest hole drilled in the name of science, where evidence of Precambrian life was found.

name: Melnikov House
description: The avant-garde design was able to bypass strict Soviet regulations on architecture.

name: Yakutsk
description: The coldest city in the world.

name: Public Museum of the Moscow Metro
description: Hidden in a metro shop under a shopping mall, this small museum tells the story of one of the world's largest urban transit systems.

name: Museum of Sin
description: A homemade anatomical collection of medical anomalies and accidental catastrophes.

name: Abandoned Marx Generator
description: A dormant lightning machine equal to all the power in Russia.

name: Bolshoi Zayatsky Island
description: No one knows why ancient labyrinths were built on this tiny northern island.

name: Monument to the Laboratory Mouse
description: This cuddly rodent has a place of honor at a Russian institute for genetic research.

name: State Darwin Museum
description: The  world's first museum dedicated to the work of game-changing naturalist Charles Darwin.

name: Gulag Museum
description: Former prison camp dedicated to the victims of political repression.

name: Kostroma Moose Farm
description: The USSR’s Conquest of Nature lead to the domestication of many moose—no squirrel.

name: Nabokov's Butterflies
description: Specimens collected by the author of "Lolita" and self-taught lepidopterist are exhibited in his childhood home.

name: F. M. Dostoyevsky Literary Memorial Museum
description: An ever-growing literary collection housed in the apartment where the Russian author penned his final novel.

name: Soviet Lifestyle Museum
description: This nostalgic Russian museum is full of USSR cultural artifacts from the 1970s and 80s.

name: The Church on the Blood
description: Church built on the site where the last tsar of Russia and his family were killed.

name: Aquarelle Train on The Moscow Metro
description: Museum design and public transportation meet in this unique Russian train car.

name: 'The Motherland Calls'
description: The world's tallest statue of a woman wields the world's tallest sword and is at risk of toppling over.

name: Grand Maket Rossiya (Grand Model of Russia)
description: A scale model of Russia so big visitors are offered binoculars to rent so they can see the whole thing.

name: Mushroom Stones of the Altai
description: Unusual, mushroom-like rock formations caused by weathering and water erosion, set to disappear, according to geologists.

name: Padlock Tree Park
description: Dozens of padlock-trees along the Moscow River.

name: Museum of Hygiene
description: This Russian museum stresses the importance of keeping clean and even holds one of Pavlov's dogs.

name: Qolşärif Mosque
description: Replica of mosque destroyed by Russia in the 16th century.

name: Mosaic Courtyard
description: A city block packed with whimsical sculptures that seem to be plucked from a tiled fantasy world.

name: Manpupuner Rock Formations
description: Gigantic natural towers in a Russian plateau.

name: The 'Golden Brains'
description: Some Moscow residents once claimed the bizarre, contorted mass of metal on top of the building was used to manipulate minds.

name: Museum of Sound
description: An unusual collection of self-made musical instruments and unique noises.

name: Whale Bone Alley
description: Siberian whale bones stand as cairns marking location of an ancient butcher yard.

name: Romanov Boyar Residence
description: Home of the future Emperors of Russia.

name: The 'Dead Road' Transpolar Railway
description: Stalin's prisoner-built rail line sits in ruin, never having a single car ride its tracks.

name: Stalin's Bunker
description: Stalin's secret bunker.

name: Lenin Museum
description: One of the last examples of Soviet architectural propaganda has a few technological tricks up its sleeve.

name: Pegrema
description: An abandoned town of skeletal wooden homes.

name: Peter the Great Statue
description: One of the world's tallest statues is also one of its most hated.

name: Gorki Leninskiye
description: Former estate of Lenin, now home to his death mask.

name: The Batagaika Crater
description: The giant Siberian crater known locally as the "doorway to the underworld" is expanding every year.

name: Monument to Yuri Gagarin
description: This massive monument honors the first human in space.

name: Moscow State University
description: Gargantuan home of Russian science.

name: Novaya Zemlya
description: Site of the world's largest nuclear explosion.

name: Independent Republic of California Embassy in Moscow
description: The California secessionist movement has its own foreign embassy, in Russia of all places.

name: Lake Labynkyr
description: This remote lake in Siberia supposedly contains a fearsome sea monster.

name: Red Gates Skyscraper
description: Stalinist skyscraper built with help of cryotechnology.

name: Diomede Islands
description: Two islands just 4 km apart separated by an International Border and Date Line.

name: Borodino Panorama
description: A 360 degree battlefield experience.

name: Pskov-Caves Monastery
description: This 15th-century cave complex is one of the very few monasteries to stay open throughout World War II and the Soviet regime.

name: Ulan-Ude Ethnographic Museum
description: A uniquely authentic display of traditional Russian culture.

name: Old English Yard
description: The "Mystery and Company of Merchant Adventurers" is an Elizabethan-era hangout in Moscow.

name: Museum of the Moscow Railway
description: This Russian locomotive museum was once devoted just to Lenin's funeral before expanding to encompass Moscow's entire train history.

name: Ganina Yama
description: Monastery complex at the location where bodies of the executed Romanov royal family were discovered.

name: Exhibition of Achievements of the National Economy
description: This newly renovated Soviet-era outdoor exhibition center is bigger than the principality of Monaco.

name: John Lennon Street
description: A small and surprising passageway in Saint Petersburg devoted to the Beatles.

name: 'Compassion'
description: A monument to Malchik, a stray dog who lived in a Moscow metro station until his tragic death.

name: Solovki
description: Monastery, army base, and infamous Soviet Gulag in the far Russian north.

name: RKK Energiya Museum
description: This iconic Russian space museum sits on a landmark space production facility and houses the Vostok 1, the first manned spacecraft, that was also built here.

name: Fort Alexander
description: A man-made island fort that has housed soldiers, scientists, and ravers.

name: Kalyazin Bell Tower
description: Stalin's march toward a more modern USSR flooded a monastery, leaving a unique and extravagant nautical marker, "The Flooded Belfry."

name: Street Art Museum
description: An art museum inside an active factory.

name: Berezovsky Mammoth
description: One of the most well-preserved mammoth specimens in the world.

name: Kondyor Massif
description: Five mile wide circular ring of rock in the Russian Far East is a curious geological oddity.

name: Chizhik Pyzhik
description: Perhaps the tiniest sculpture in Saint Petersburg is a little bronze finch inspired by a song.

name: Pavlov's House
description: The Nazis lost more men trying to take this Russian-held Stalingrad apartment block than they did when taking Paris.

name: Davsha
description: What was once the base for Russia's first nature reserve is now a ghost town, right on the shore of Lake Baikal.

name: Bunker-703
description: A former special storage facility for documents during the Cold War.

name: The Church of Our Lady of Kazan
description: An unusual abandoned church damaged in World War II sits in a small village outside Moscow.

name: Ivolginsky Datsan
description: The mummified remains of Dashi-Dorzho Itigilov, Russia's most important Buddhist.

name: Arctic Siberian Campion Plant Revived
description: Russian researchers who have been excavating ancient squirrel burrows may have brought a 32,000-year-old plant back to life.

name: Keyboard Monument
description: Russia's giant concrete QWERTY keyboard is likely the largest in the world.

name: Tula Pryanik Museum
description: A tea party concludes each visit to this Russian spice cookie sanctum.

name: Giant Lenin Head of Ulan-Ude
description: This massive glowering Lenin head gazes out over eastern Siberia.

name: The Wax Museum
description: An incredibly random collection of wax figures is displayed in a shopping center walkway.

name: Mount Elbrus
description: Europe's highest peak is a key pillar of the Seven Summits mountaineering challenge.

name: The Kuril Islands
description: Thanks to this small string of islands World War II still hasn't officially ended between Russia and Japan.

name: Ekranoplan Lun
description: An enormous Soviet aircraft, the only one of its kind, stands at the deserted shore of the Caspian Sea.

name: Oreshek Fortress
description: An abandoned 14th-century castle and prison complex on a small island in the Neva River.

name: Gamsutl
description: Propped high atop the peak of Mount Gamsutlmeer, this abandoned village is one of the oldest settlements in the region.

name: Museum of Wooden Architecture and Peasant Life
description: 300-year-old villagers houses and traditional churches preserve a nearly lost piece of Russian history.

name: Lenin's Hut
description: A replica of the straw hut where Vladimir Lenin lived in hiding, disguised as a hay farmer, in 1917.

name: Birobidzhan
description: Fascinating early attempt at a Jewish autonomous settlement.

name: Alyosha Statue
description: A monument to the defenders of the Arctic in Murmansk, Russia.

name: Russian Museum of Military Medicine
description: Rare documents, old instruments, and gruesome specimens showcase the history of military medicine.

name: The Kamera (The Chamber)
description: The laboratory where the Soviet secret police invented exotic poisons used to kill dissidents in hideous and (mostly) untraceable ways.

name: Assumption Cathedral and Monastery
description: A former military outpost of Ivan the Terrible, the 16th-century church contains some of the rarest examples of Eastern Orthodox mural paintings.

name: Castle of Childhood
description: A magnificent, Disneyesque chateau built for 150 lucky kindergarteners.

name: House of Evgeny Smolik
description: A self-taught Siberian craftsman creates a fantastical  and personal design for his 19th century village house.

name: Vavilov Research Institute of Plant Industry
description: Inside the world's largest seed bank, WWII botanists elected to starve to death rather than compromise their collection.

name: Oleg's Mound
description: This burial mound is said to be the final resting place of Russia's first Viking ruler, Oleg of Novogrod.

name: Muzey Kino
description: For lovers of Soviet cinema, this museum is home to a vast collection of artifacts and costumes.

name: Nazino Island
description: Social outcasts and cannibalism in Stalin's Soviet Union.

name: Svetlogorsk Beach Gondolas
description: These tiny cabins on a cableway have been carrying visitors at a Baltic resort down to the beach since 1912.

name: Levashovo Memorial Cemetery
description: Pictures tacked onto trees remember the more than 40,000 of Stalin's victims buried here in mass graves.

name: Patriot Park
description: You'll find rocket launchers, not rollercoasters, at Russia's “military Disneyland.”

name: Main Cathedral of the Russian Armed Forces
description: A solemn, monumental, kitschy palace of worship honoring victory in the Great Patriotic War.

name: Suyumbike Tower
description: Russia's own leaning tower was built on a tragic historical fable.

name: Mysterious Volgograd Balls
description: Volcanic irregularity? Odd erosion? Alien eggs? Local legends compete to explain these strange orbs.

name: Chess City
description: The largest Buddhist city in Europe is a haven for chess fanatics.

name: Nikita's Guesthouse on Olkhon Island
description: Intricate wood carvings on an island in Siberia.

name: Pushkin Library Metro Station
description: A "station to nowhere" marks the site of a Siberian subway project that never saw the light of day.

name: Cathedral of Saint Demetrius
description: Thousands of biblical and pagan images are carved into this small 12th-century church.

name: Alexander Golod's Pyramids
description: Ukrainian defense contractor's center for studying "pyramid energy."

name: Dacha Kvitko
description: An abandoned mansion is now a haven for urban explorers.

name: Tchaikovsky State House Museum
description: The composer did some of his finest work in this country home, including "The Nutcracker."

name: Dacha Stalina
description: One of the notorious Soviet leader Joseph Stalin's many summer retreats.

name: Beatles Monument
description: Mostly underground in the old days Back in the USSR, today the Beatles beckon to tourists from a wall sculpture out in the open in central Russia.

name: Por-Bajin
description: The Uyghur settlement in the middle of a lake continues to raise questions.

name: Museum Retro Park
description: A tiny park off the beaten path covered in ingenious scrap metal sculptures.

name: Shukhov Tower on the Oka River
description: An unused hyberbolic electricity pylon kept as architectural monument.

name: Tiberkul Religious Settlement
description: Home of Vissarion, the "Jesus of Siberia."

name: Urup Island
description: Is it an island or a volcano? That question probably explains why it's uninhabited.

name: Fyodor Dostoevsky Theater of Dramatic Art
description: This late-brutalist masterpiece everyone forgot about was originally created to get the proletariat jazzed about the arts.

name: Rio Shipwreck
description: One of the most popular attractions in the small resort town of Kabardinka.

name: AvtoVAZ Museum
description: A collection of iconic Soviet car models on display.

name: Chinese Village at Tsarskoe Selo
description: A model village fit for an emperor.

name: A.N. Scriabin Memorial Museum
description: A museum dedicated to controversial Russian pianist Alexander Scriabin.

name: Nikola-Lenivets Art Park
description: The largest art park on the European continent.

name: 'Worker and Kolkhoz Woman'
description: An iconic Soviet statue of working-class people.

name: Krenitsyn Volcano
description: A remote and picturesque volcano nestled inside another volcano.

name: Tula Museum of Samovars
description: A Russian museum dedicated to the household staple, the samovar.

name: Vologda Butter Museum
description: Slather on the history at an exhibition dedicated to this Russian luxury.

name: Home Museum of Wooden Sculptures
description: A curated museum of wooden sculptures inhabits this Russian home.

name: Eagle Cliffs
description: These limestone cliffs are associated with the Greek myth of Prometheus.

name: Rinpoche Bagsha Datsan
description: Located along Lysaya Mountain, this monastery is home to the largest statue of Buddha in Russia.

name: Mask of Sorrow
description: This eerie sculpture overlooks what was known as the Road of Bones under Joseph Stalin.

name: Chelyabinsk Meteorite
description: The largest-known fragment of an asteroid that exploded over the southern Ural region in 2013.

name: Monument to Enemas
description: Russian spa's bronze monument to enemas.

name: Lake Karachay
description: Most polluted lake in the world, used as a radioactive dumping ground for years.

name: Sukko Cypress Lake
description: This cypress grove is a unique site considering these trees are not native to the region.

name: Bolghar
description: During the Soviet era, the medieval capital of Volga Bulgaria served as a substitute for Mecca.

name: Tashanta
description: The last stop in southeast Russia.

name: Monument to Peter I
description: This effigy of Peter the Great is greatly disproportionate.

name: Fort No. 5 "Friedrich Wilhelm III"
description: This monument commemorates the soldiers who fought for 16 hours straight to penetrate this concrete fort.

name: Feynman Diagram Rock Carving
description: A tribute to Richard Feynman is carved into the land he planned to see but never got to visit.

name: Goliath Transmitter
description: The Russians still use the transmitter Nazis built during World War II to communicate with submarines.

name: Piskaryovskoye Memorial Cemetery
description: This memorial complex contains several mass graves for victims of the Siege of Leningrad.

name: Dudinka CHAYKA-Mast
description: Unused mast taller than every structure in the EU.

name: Kronshtadt Sea Gauge
description: This historic system is the zero level benchmark for measuring the depths and altitudes of the Baltic.

name: Geyser Lake
description: Thermal springs at the bottom of this lake create beautiful blue circles of clay.

name: 'Yoshkin Cat'
description: A smug-looking cat representing an humorous Russian expletive.

name: Russkiy Levsha (The Russian Lefty Museum)
description: Home to artworks so small you need a microscope to see them.

name: Raketa Watch Factory
description: An iconic Russian watch brand inspired by Yuri Gagarin's trip to outer space.

name: Ulakhan-Sis Range
description: A remote Arctic mountain range is home to "granite cities" found almost nowhere else.

name: House of Soviets
description: Dominating the skyline of Kaliningrad, the iconic House of Soviets has always been abandoned.

name: Yul Brynner Monument
description: A tribute to a Hollywood icon far from Tinseltown.

name: The Museum of Water
description: Immersive exhibitions track Saint Petersburg’s past and present.

name: Ticket to the USSR
description: This small museum is basically a time capsule of daily life in the Soviet Union.

name: Institute of Russian Realist Art
description: One of the largest collections of socialist realist art shows the surprising depth of the official art of the Soviet Union.

name: Nelson's Courtyard
description: A surreal pile of colorful junk fills a small yard in an otherwise gray neighborhood.

name: Twin Stars Diner
description: This Russian diner is staffed entirely by twins.

name: Sutyagin House.
description: What once claimed to be the world's tallest single-family wooden structure house.
